arguments.description:
## Capability (API landed)
`TicketStore::release_lease(ticket_id, requester)` added in memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store/board.rs with the semantics:
- the holder (`working_by == requester`) may always release its own lease;
- any caller may release a stale (expired) lease;
- a live lease held by a different agent is rejected with `LeaseConflict`;
- releasing a ticket with no active lease is a no-op.
Unit test `release_lease_enforces_owner_and_stale_rules` covers all four cases (passing).

## Remaining work
1. Surface `release_lease` across all transports (parity): ticket-cli (`release`/rework `unclaim` to clear orphaned/stale leases even without a board entry), ticket-mcp tool, ticket-http route. Reconcile with the existing board check-in/out flow.
2. Fix the orphaned-lease bug that motivated this: a lease can outlive its board entry (observed on 82d6ada4 — `board check-out` reports "no active board entry" while `list_leases` still shows the lease). board_check_out should release the lease even when the board entry is already gone.
3. Clear the stale `copilot` lease on 82d6ada4-ac35-45a7-9df6-7b7501d58e70 (root store) once a transport surface exists.

## Acceptance criteria
- release_lease is invokable from CLI, MCP, and HTTP with identical semantics.
- board check-out never leaves an orphaned lease.
- The 82d6ada4 orphaned lease is cleared.
arguments.title: [ticket-api] Lease-release capability — owner/stale release semantics + transport parity + orphaned-lease cleanup

events.reasoning_text:
Looking at the viewer-api changes, I've got three deleted retro tickets to stage, but I need to skip the .gitignore modification since that's from other work. For memory-api, I'm tracking changes to board.rs and workflow_tests.rs along with updates to the ticket and spec stores.

At the root level, there's a deleted retro ticket (be01536d) that needs staging. I've also created a new lease ticket (ca7e0388).

For the commit order, I need to think through the hierarchy — viewer-api and memory-api are both submodules of the root repository, so I'll commit those first, then the root with the pointer updates and the deleted ticket.

The staging plan for viewer-api is to add the three deleted audit-roadmap tickets without touching .gitignore. For memory-api, I'm staging the board.rs and workflow_tests.rs code changes, the new .ticket and .spec directories, the deleted retro folders, and all the new tickets from this session — but I need to be careful to exclude Cargo.lock and .session files.

Since .ticket/tickets contains untracked folders and the index is gitignored, staging `.ticket/tickets` and `.spec/specs` explicitly should safely capture the new and deleted tickets plus spec files along with the two code changes. Then for root, I'll stage the deleted ticket and update the submodule pointers.

But I need to check if I already bumped the root pointer to memory-api after that first checkpoint commit — I only committed inside memory-api, so root's pointer is still behind by a couple of commits.  Bumping it now will capture both changes, which is what I want. I should also verify whether the pre-commit hook fires on these submodule commits — the hook is at root level checking for rule-managed files, and none of my staged changes are rule files, so it shouldn't interfere.

For the memory-api ticket store, I'm committing all the new tickets from this session (the content-materialization epic, ticket-api tickets, lease ticket, and specs) plus the four deleted retro folders — that's the full scope of the work. Though there's a possibility of background churn from other agents mixed in, which I should account for.

Let me check the full memory-api status to see what's actually staged before committing, then I'll do viewer-api first since it's clean, inspect memory-api carefully, and work through the commits in order.

Looking at the viewer-api changes, I've got three deleted retro tickets to stage, but I need to skip the .gitignore modification since that's from other work. For memory-api, I'm tracking changes to board.rs and workflow_tests.rs along with updates to the ticket and spec stores.

At the root level, there's a deleted retro ticket (be01536d) that needs staging. I've also created a new lease ticket (ca7e0388).

For the commit order, I need to think through the hierarchy — viewer-api and memory-api are both submodules of the root repository, so I'll commit those first, then the root with the pointer updates and the deleted ticket.

The staging plan for viewer-api is to add the three deleted audit-roadmap tickets without touching .gitignore. For memory-api, I'm staging the board.rs and workflow_tests.rs code changes, the new .ticket and .spec directories, the deleted retro folders, and all the new tickets from this session — but I need to be careful to exclude Cargo.lock and .session files.

Since .ticket/tickets contains untracked folders and the index is gitignored, staging `.ticket/tickets` and `.spec/specs` explicitly should safely capture the new and deleted tickets plus spec files along with the two code changes. Then for root, I'll stage the deleted ticket and update the submodule pointers.

But I need to check if I already bumped the root pointer to memory-api after that first checkpoint commit — I only committed inside memory-api, so root's pointer is still behind by a couple of commits.  Bumping it now will capture both changes, which is what I want. I should also verify whether the pre-commit hook fires on these submodule commits — the hook is at root level checking for rule-managed files, and none of my staged changes are rule files, so it shouldn't interfere.

For the memory-api ticket store, I'm committing all the new tickets from this session (the content-materialization epic, ticket-api tickets, lease ticket, and specs) plus the four deleted retro folders — that's the full scope of the work. Though there's a possibility of background churn from other agents mixed in, which I should account for.

Let me check the full memory-api status to see what's actually staged before committing, then I'll do viewer-api first since it's clean, inspect memory-api carefully, and work through the commits in order.

events.reasoning_text:
I've identified the clean changes in memory-api—just my two code files without any extraneous build artifacts or session files—so I can safely stage the board and workflow test files along with the new tickets I've created.

Now I'm verifying that all 22 ticket/spec changes are actually mine by cross-referencing the session IDs: my epic plus five children, two retrofix tickets, three ticket-api health tickets, and a lease ticket totals twelve new tickets, plus four deleted retro entries and some description updates. With each new ticket generating multiple files (ticket.toml, description.md, and history), that accounts for the line count in the status output, so I'm confident staging the `.ticket` and `.spec` directories along with the code changes captures everything I've worked on. Staging `.ticket/tickets` and `.spec/specs` is safe since the gitignored index/db files live elsewhere under `.ticket/`. I'm debating whether to make this a single comprehensive checkpoint or split it into separate commits for the lease code versus the ticket/spec store — the user wants a full checkpoint, so one commit makes sense even if it mixes concerns.

Actually, splitting into two memory-api commits would be cleaner: one for the board and workflow changes with the lease semantics, another for the ticket and spec materialization. Then I'll follow with a root commit for the deletion and submodule pointer updates. Let me go with the two-commit approach.

Removes value-less audit-roadmap orphan-convergence collection tickets (batch-2 chunks)." 2>&1 | tail -6

arguments.goal: Commit memory-api code and plan changes

**3. ## Handoff 1 — Implement `release_lease` across all transports

~~~text
# Handoff — Wire `release_lease` into CLI/MCP/HTTP and clear the orphaned lease

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend
- `LEASE-TICKET` → ca7e0388-ee5c-41c7-8385-7de6817ce261 — "[ticket-api] Lease-release capability — owner/stale release semantics + transport parity + orphaned-lease cleanup"
- `ORPHAN-TICKET` → 82d6ada4-ac35-45a7-9df6-7b7501d58e70 — root-store ticket carrying the stale `copilot` lease that must be cleared
- `<memory-api>` → C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api (the owning ticket store for LEASE-TICKET)

## Overview
The core lease-release capability has already landed: `TicketStore::release_lease(ticket_id, requester)` in [board.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store/board.rs) with a passing unit test. What remains is transport parity (CLI/MCP/HTTP), fixing the `board_check_out` bug that lets a lease outlive its board entry, and actually clearing the orphaned `copilot` lease on ORPHAN-TICKET. Start in `<memory-api>`. This is a self-contained ticket-api track; it unblocks board hygiene used by every other track.

## Findings
- `release_lease` semantics (already implemented and tested): holder (`working_by == requester`) may always release; any caller may release a stale/expired lease; a live lease held by a different agent → `StorageError::LeaseConflict`; no active lease → no-op.
- The motivating bug: a lease can outlive its board entry. Observed on ORPHAN-TICKET — `board check-out` reports "no active board entry" while `list_leases()` still shows the lease. `board_check_out` must release the lease even when the board entry is already gone.
- `board_check_in` creates both a board entry and a lease (via `claim`); `unclaim` historically removed leases unconditionally. Reconcile the new owner/stale-aware path with this existing flow rather than duplicating it.
- Transport surfaces to touch already exist for `unclaim` and can host `release`: CLI command plumbing, MCP board tools, and HTTP error mapping already handle `LeaseConflict`.

## Decisions
- Keep API semantics as shipped; do not loosen owner/stale rules at the transport layer — transports call through to `release_lease` for identical behavior.
- Prefer reworking/extending the existing `unclaim` path to clear orphaned/stale leases without requiring a board entry, over inventing a parallel command, unless a distinct `release` verb reads more clearly.

## Blockers
- None hard. ORPHAN-TICKET cannot be cleared until at least one transport surface exposes `release_lease`; do the CLI first so the clear can be executed immediately.

## Key Entities
- [LEASE-TICKET description](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/ca7e0388-ee5c-41c7-8385-7de6817ce261/description.md) — authoritative remaining-work list and acceptance criteria.
- [board.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store/board.rs) — `release_lease`, `unclaim`, `claim`; the core method to expose.
- [workflow_tests.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/tests/workflow_tests.rs) — `release_lease_enforces_owner_and_stale_rules` (pattern to extend for check-out orphan fix).
- [error.rs](memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/error.rs) — `LeaseConflict { ticket, holder }` variant.
- [lifecycle.rs](memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli/commands/lifecycle.rs) — `cmd_unclaim`; CLI entry to extend.
- [core.rs](memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli/args/core.rs) — `UnclaimArgs` (add `--stale`/`--force` or a `release` arg struct).
- [cli.rs](memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli.rs) and [dispatch.rs](memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli/dispatch.rs) — command registration + dry-run payload.
- [ticket-http serve/error.rs](memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/error.rs) — `LeaseConflict` → HTTP status mapping (already present; add the route).
- [ticket-cli README](memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/README.md) and [ticket-mcp README](memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/README.md) — surfaces to document the new capability.

## Long-Horizon Goal
LEASE-TICKET closed: `release_lease` invokable identically from CLI, MCP, and HTTP; `board check-out` never leaves an orphaned lease; ORPHAN-TICKET's stale lease cleared.

## Next Actions (execute in order)
1. Wire the CLI surface: extend `cmd_unclaim`/args (or add a `release` verb) to call `release_lease`, including clearing orphaned/stale leases with no board entry. Register in `cli.rs`, `dispatch.rs`, and batch dispatch.
2. First validation check: `cargo test -p ticket-api --lib` (expect 110 pass; the pre-existing `storage::move_planner::tests::preflight_reports_invisible_reference_visibility_and_path_refs` failure is unrelated — do not absorb it) plus a CLI integration test in [integration_workflow_crud.rs](memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/tests/integration_workflow_crud.rs).
3. Fix the orphan bug: make `board_check_out` release the lease even when the board entry is already gone; add a regression test mirroring the observed ORPHAN-TICKET scenario.
4. Wire the MCP tool (ticket-mcp board module) and the HTTP route (ticket-http) to `release_lease`; assert identical semantics across all three.
5. Clear ORPHAN-TICKET's stale `copilot` lease via the new CLI surface against the root store; confirm `list_leases()` no longer shows it.
6. Update ticket-cli and ticket-mcp READMEs; move LEASE-TICKET to `in-review`, then `done` after review-gate checklist.

## Completion Status
1. CLI wiring — not-started
2. CLI/lib validation — not-started
3. `board_check_out` orphan fix — not-started
4. MCP + HTTP parity — not-started
5. Clear ORPHAN-TICKET lease — not-started (blocked on step 1)
6. Docs + close LEASE-TICKET — not-started

## Definition Of Done
`release_lease` callable with identical owner/stale semantics from CLI, MCP, and HTTP; `board check-out` provably never orphans a lease (regression test); ORPHAN-TICKET lease cleared and verified absent from `list_leases`; ticket-api lib + CLI integration tests green (modulo the documented unrelated move_planner failure); READMEs updated; LEASE-TICKET closed through the review gate.

## Board / Session Notes
- Commit deepest-first (ticket-api core → tools → store), staging owned files only; exclude `Cargo.lock`, `.session/`, and the dirty `memory-viewers` submodule.
- The `move_planner` preflight test failure is pre-existing (handoff Finding #5 in the originating session) — keep it documented as unrelated, never silently fixed or hidden.

Execute the next actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.
~~~

# Handoff — Review the content-materialization epic (3be95a71) end-to-end

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend

  - `G-E` → 7f7ef435-0504-40cf-8d6c-dd96477a0223 — "[content-materialization][workspace] G-E: De-submodularize — real dependency imports (gated)"
  - `SESSION-EPIC` → effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8 — "[session-bootstrap][epic] Dynamic session bootstrapping & context routing redesign"
  - `HEALTH-PROMOTE` → c680b137-01a1-4d13-b219-39af86eaa71b — "[ticket-api] Promote health_check to a ticket-api call with CLI/MCP/HTTP transport parity"
  - `XSTORE-RESOLVE` → f3a58d3c-5961-4ee4-a32d-b51343a5a275 — "[ticket-api] Dangling-edge health check must resolve cross-store URN references"
- G-B retro-fix children:
  - `G-B.1` → 7c281aec-092c-448d-bf26-94522e07b4e8 — typed MoveError variant (replace string marker)
  - `G-B.2` → fa6e2c19-5fd8-4305-90c7-f1f4bea8df36 — trait-based interoperability contract
- HEALTH-PROMOTE child:
  - `HEALTH-GUARD` → 835b332b-8c1f-4423-bf21-4fac7aa7c8f7 — "[ticket-api] Health + state-guard: stop warning on ready→ready; flag/guard only tickets progressed ahead of dependencies" (state `ready`; health fix + state guard already implemented and committed)

## Overview
EPIC is a materialized 8-child program layered on top of the session-bootstrap epic. The prior session created the structure, wired dependency edges, and landed the health/state-guard work under HEALTH-PROMOTE. This handoff is a review pass: verify each child's spec/description completeness, confirm the dependency edges reflect real prerequisites, run health across the subtree, and flag any node that is under-specified or mis-linked before implementation ramps up. Start in `<memory-api>`; query EPIC and children from that store (aggregated `default` reads work, but `get`/`get_ticket_description` resolve reliably against `<memory-api>`).

## Findings
- EPIC subgraph: 61-node/111-edge reach at depth 3 in the originating survey; direct depth-1 children are the 8 listed above. All EPIC-direct nodes are `state = new` except SESSION-EPIC.
- `XSTORE-RESOLVE` is intentionally linked as a prerequisite ahead of the content work — dangling-edge false positives (targets like 82d6ada4, 6bd67a7a, f8b447b7 live in the root store, not `<memory-api>`) block clean health signal until cross-store URN resolution lands.
- `HEALTH-GUARD` is functionally complete: `unblocked_with_deps` finding removed from all four sites and the `enforce_dependency_progress` state guard + typed `DependencyNotProgressed` error committed. Confirm its ticket state advances toward `done` rather than lingering at `ready`.
- G-B has two concrete retro-fix children (`G-B.1`, `G-B.2`) that replace string-marker/runtime validation with typed + trait-based contracts — these are the most implementation-ready nodes and good review anchors for whether children carry enough detail to start.
- Nodes with `title: null` in graph output (82d6ada4, f8b447b7, 6bd67a7a) are cross-store references into the root store — expected, not corruption; they are exactly what XSTORE-RESOLVE addresses.

## Decisions
- Do NOT prune the "dangling" edges — they are valid cross-store dependencies; the fix is resolution, not deletion.
- Bulk-moving the tight cross-store cluster to memory-api is deferred until XSTORE-RESOLVE lands and the stale lease on 82d6ada4 is cleared (see Handoff 1). Keep EPIC in `<memory-api>`; migrate only genuinely multi-repo planning to the root store.

## Blockers
- Clean subtree health cannot be asserted until XSTORE-RESOLVE eliminates cross-store false positives; treat current dangling_edge findings on 82d6ada4/6bd67a7a/f8b447b7 as known-benign during review.

## Key Entities
- [EPIC description](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c/description.md) — program intent and acceptance framing.
- [G-B.1 description](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/7c281aec-092c-448d-bf26-94522e07b4e8/description.md) and [G-B.2 description](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/fa6e2c19-5fd8-4305-90c7-f1f4bea8df36/description.md) — most implementation-ready children; use as the completeness benchmark.
- [HEALTH-GUARD description](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/835b332b-8c1f-4423-bf21-4fac7aa7c8f7/description.md) — verify state matches the completed implementation.
- [health.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/health.rs) — canonical `collect_findings`; confirm HEALTH-PROMOTE's transport-dedup scope is still accurate.
- [store.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store.rs) — `enforce_dependency_progress` guard landed under HEALTH-GUARD.
- Prompts to reuse: [ticket-system instructions](.agents/instructions/ticket-system.instructions.md), [spec-system instructions](.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md), [tickets prompt](.agents/prompts/tickets.prompt.md).

## Long-Horizon Goal
EPIC reviewed and made execution-ready: every child has a complete, testable description with correct dependency edges; subtree health is clean (modulo documented cross-store items); the first implementable child (G-B.1) can be picked up without reconstruction.

## Next Actions (execute in order)
1. Pull EPIC + all 8 direct children full descriptions from `<memory-api>` and read them; list any child missing acceptance criteria, motivation, or effort.
2. First validation check: run `mcp_ticket-mcp_health_check` (or `ticket health`) rooted at EPIC (`root=3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c`, `direction=out`) and classify each finding as real vs known cross-store false positive.
3. Verify dependency-direction correctness: EPIC `depends_on` its children; children depend on sibling prerequisites (e.g. `G-E → G-A/G-B/G-C/G-D`, `633a38b8 → c680b137`); flag any inverted or missing edge with a `--reason`.
4. Confirm the two committed sub-tracks reflect reality: advance HEALTH-GUARD state if the implementation is done; confirm HEALTH-PROMOTE/XSTORE-RESOLVE scopes still match code.
5. For each under-specified child, add the missing description fields (acceptance criteria, effort, motivation) so it is pick-up-ready; do not implement yet.
6. Produce a short review verdict per child (ready / needs-detail / blocked) and recommend the first implementable node (expected: G-B.1 7c281aec or XSTORE-RESOLVE f3a58d3c).

## Completion Status
1. Read all child descriptions — not-started
2. Subtree health run + triage — not-started
3. Edge-direction audit — not-started
4. Sub-track state reconciliation — not-started
5. Fill under-specified children — not-started
6. Per-child review verdict — not-started

## Definition Of Done
Every EPIC child has a complete, testable description and correct dependency edges; subtree health run is triaged with cross-store items explicitly marked benign; committed sub-tracks (HEALTH-GUARD/HEALTH-PROMOTE/XSTORE-RESOLVE) have states matching their implementation; a written ready/needs-detail/blocked verdict exists per child with a recommended first implementable node.

## Board / Session Notes

Execute the next actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.

## Overview
EPIC is a materialized 8-child program layered on top of the session-bootstrap epic. The prior session created the structure, wired dependency edges, and landed the health/state-guard work under HEALTH-PROMOTE. This handoff is a review pass: verify each child's spec/description completeness, confirm the dependency edges reflect real prerequisites, run health across the subtree, and flag any node that is under-specified or mis-linked before implementation ramps up. Start in `<memory-api>`; query EPIC and children from that store (aggregated `default` reads work, but `get`/`get_ticket_description` resolve reliably against `<memory-api>`).

## Findings
- EPIC subgraph: 61-node/111-edge reach at depth 3 in the originating survey; direct depth-1 children are the 8 listed above. All EPIC-direct nodes are `state = new` except SESSION-EPIC.
- `XSTORE-RESOLVE` is intentionally linked as a prerequisite ahead of the content work — dangling-edge false positives (targets like 82d6ada4, 6bd67a7a, f8b447b7 live in the root store, not `<memory-api>`) block clean health signal until cross-store URN resolution lands.
- `HEALTH-GUARD` is functionally complete: `unblocked_with_deps` finding removed from all four sites and the `enforce_dependency_progress` state guard + typed `DependencyNotProgressed` error committed. Confirm its ticket state advances toward `done` rather than lingering at `ready`.
- G-B has two concrete retro-fix children (`G-B.1`, `G-B.2`) that replace string-marker/runtime validation with typed + trait-based contracts — these are the most implementation-ready nodes and good review anchors for whether children carry enough detail to start.
- Nodes with `title: null` in graph output (82d6ada4, f8b447b7, 6bd67a7a) are cross-store references into the root store — expected, not corruption; they are exactly what XSTORE-RESOLVE addresses.

## Decisions
- Do NOT prune the "dangling" edges — they are valid cross-store dependencies; the fix is resolution, not deletion.
- Bulk-moving the tight cross-store cluster to memory-api is deferred until XSTORE-RESOLVE lands and the stale lease on 82d6ada4 is cleared (see Handoff 1). Keep EPIC in `<memory-api>`; migrate only genuinely multi-repo planning to the root store.

## Blockers

## Review Verdict — EPIC `3be95a71` content-materialization

**The good news first, because I'm feeling generous:** the previous session did *not* leave you a dumpster fire. Descriptions have real motivation, deliverables, and acceptance criteria. Edge directions are actually correct — a rarity. Nobody inverted the epic→children dependency like a junior copy-pasting a graph tutorial. Credit given.

**Now the parts that were quietly rotting:**

### Health status
| Before | After |
|---|---|
| 10 findings (5 dangling_edge + 5 missing_effort) | **5 findings, all known-benign cross-store danglers** |

Fixed: effort set on `c680b137` (2500), `f3a58d3c` (1800), `b03be2d5` (2000), `f00291a3` (2000), `835b332b` (1200). The remaining 5 danglers (`82d6ada4`/`f8b447b7`/`6bd67a7a`) are exactly what XSTORE-RESOLVE targets — **do not prune them**.

| Child | Verdict | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **G-A** `633a38b8` | ✅ ready | 5 sections, dogfood target, `→ HEALTH-PROMOTE` edge correct |
| **G-B** `17483930` | ✅ ready | properly decomposed into G-B.1/G-B.2 |
| **G-B.1** `7c281aec` | ✅ **ready — pick this first** | exact files, typed-variant target, effort 1500. Zero reconstruction needed. |
| **G-B.2** `fa6e2c19` | ✅ ready | trait-contract, effort 2500 |
| **G-C** `6875dff6` | ✅ ready | leaf, no prereq needed |

Flagging, not auto-adding — those targets live in other subtrees and blind-linking would just spawn more cross-store danglers before XSTORE-RESOLVE lands. |
| **G-E** `7f7ef435` | ✅ ready (gated) | `→ G-A/B/C/D` gating correct |
| **HEALTH-PROMOTE** `c680b137` | ✅ ready | scope still matches health.rs; triplication real |
| **XSTORE-RESOLVE** `f3a58d3c` | ✅ ready — **or pick this first** | prerequisite that unblocks clean health |

Reconciled to DONE, advanced `ready → in-review`. Left `done` behind a fresh `cargo test -p ticket-api` gate. |

### Recommended first implementable node
**G-B.1 `7c281aec`** — smallest, fully-specified, exact file targets. If you want to unblock the health signal instead, **XSTORE-RESOLVE `f3a58d3c`** is the higher-leverage prerequisite.

Bump it yourself when you're ready: `git add memory-api && git commit -m "chore: bump memory-api pointer"`.

release_lease has landed:

### Overview
This track is implementation-complete and committed: lease release semantics are now exposed across CLI, MCP, and HTTP; `board_check_out` now cleans orphaned leases even when the board entry is already gone; the orphaned stale lease on ticket `82d6ada4-ac35-45a7-9df6-7b7501d58e70` was cleared and verified absent from lease listings. Two commits were created: `3ac9718` in `memory-api` and `6acc17c8` in the parent repo (submodule pointer update).

### Shorthand And Placeholder Legend
- `LEASE-TICKET` -> `ca7e0388-ee5c-41c7-8385-7de6817ce261` (`[ticket-api] Lease-release capability — owner/stale release semantics + transport parity + orphaned-lease cleanup`)
- `ORPHAN-TICKET` -> `82d6ada4-ac35-45a7-9df6-7b7501d58e70`
- `<workspace-root>` -> [./](./)
- `<memory-api>` -> [./memory-api](./memory-api)

### Ticket Legend
- `LEASE-TICKET` -> `ca7e0388-ee5c-41c7-8385-7de6817ce261` (`[ticket-api] Lease-release capability — owner/stale release semantics + transport parity + orphaned-lease cleanup`)
- `ORPHAN-TICKET` -> `82d6ada4-ac35-45a7-9df6-7b7501d58e70` (root-store ticket that previously carried a stale `copilot` lease)

### Findings
- `release_lease` owner/stale semantics were already present and retained; transports now call through consistently.
- The orphaning bug was real: `board_check_out` could fail on missing board entry and leave the lease behind.
- CLI `unclaim` previously depended on active board entry context; it now also releases leases directly with requester-aware semantics.
- HTTP had no explicit lease-release endpoint; MCP had no explicit lease-release board tool.
- Root-store lease count dropped from `26` to `25` after clearing `ORPHAN-TICKET`, and `ORPHAN-TICKET` disappeared from `leases` output.

### Decisions
- Kept API semantics strict (holder release always allowed; stale release allowed by any requester; live other-holder returns conflict).
- Implemented transport parity by wiring existing `release_lease` rather than inventing divergent transport-only logic.
- Fixed orphan behavior in `board_check_out` by always attempting lease release, even when board completion fails.

### Blockers
- No hard blockers for this track.
- Known unrelated failure still present in lib sweep: `storage::move_planner::tests::preflight_reports_invisible_reference_visibility_and_path_refs`.

### Long-Horizon Goal
- Close and keep closed lease-orphan hygiene for ticket-api transports: all lease-release semantics remain identical across CLI/MCP/HTTP, and board exit paths do not leak stale leases.

### Next Actions (Execution Order)
1. First concrete action: verify current dirty state before any additional work, especially unrelated files in `memory-api` ticket `835b332b-8c1f-4423-bf21-4fac7aa7c8f7`.
2. First validation check: run `cargo test -p ticket-api --lib` in [./memory-api](./memory-api) and expect `110 passed` with the one known unrelated move-planner failure unless independently fixed.
3. If shipping/pushing: push `memory-api` commit `3ac9718`, then push parent repo commit `6acc17c8`.
4. Optional hygiene follow-up: evaluate whether to clear additional stale leases in root store now that release path is available.

### Completion Status
- CLI wiring to `release_lease`: `completed` (including orphan/no-board-entry path via `unclaim`).
- CLI/lib validation: `completed` (focused tests passed; lib run matched known unrelated failure profile).
- `board_check_out` orphan fix: `completed` (store logic updated + regression test added).
- MCP parity: `completed` (`board_release_lease` tool added and surfaced in help/docs).
- HTTP parity: `completed` (new `release-lease` route + tests).
- ORPHAN lease clear: `completed` (`82d6ada4-ac35-45a7-9df6-7b7501d58e70` removed from lease list).
- Docs and ticket closure: `completed` (`LEASE-TICKET` moved through `in-review` to `done`).

### Key Entities
- Lease core behavior: [./memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store/board.rs](./memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store/board.rs) (source of truth for claim/unclaim/release/check-out logic).
- Store regressions: [./memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/tests/workflow_tests.rs](./memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/tests/workflow_tests.rs) (owner/stale + orphan check-out cleanup coverage).
- CLI args and release path: [./memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli/args/core.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli/args/core.rs), [./memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli/commands/lifecycle.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli/commands/lifecycle.rs).
- CLI integration regression: [./memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/tests/integration_workflow_crud.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/tests/integration_workflow_crud.rs).
- MCP parity wiring: [./memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/types.rs](./memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/types.rs), [./memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/board.rs](./memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/board.rs), [./memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server.rs](./memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server.rs), [./memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/workflow.rs](./memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/workflow.rs).
- HTTP parity wiring: [./memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/routes.rs](./memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/routes.rs), [./memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/tickets/mutations.rs](./memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/tickets/mutations.rs), [./memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/tickets/types.rs](./memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/tickets/types.rs), [./memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/tickets/tests/mutations.rs](./memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/tickets/tests/mutations.rs).
- Docs updated: [./memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/README.md](./memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/README.md), [./memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/README.md](./memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/README.md).
- Ticket records:
  - `LEASE-TICKET`: [./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/ca7e0388-ee5c-41c7-8385-7de6817ce261/ticket.toml](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/ca7e0388-ee5c-41c7-8385-7de6817ce261/ticket.toml)
  - `ORPHAN-TICKET`: [./.ticket/tickets/82d6ada4-ac35-45a7-9df6-7b7501d58e70/ticket.toml](./.ticket/tickets/82d6ada4-ac35-45a7-9df6-7b7501d58e70/ticket.toml)

### Commit Summary
- `memory-api` commit: `3ac9718` — `fix(ticket-api): wire lease release across cli mcp http and cleanup orphans`
- Parent repo commit: `6acc17c8` — `chore: update memory-api submodule pointer for lease-release parity`

### Board / Persisted Session Notes
- No active board entries were required to finish this track.
- Unrelated dirty state remains outside this change set:
  - root workspace has `.session/*`, `Cargo.lock`, and unrelated submodule dirt.
  - `memory-api` still has unrelated modifications under ticket `835b332b-8c1f-4423-bf21-4fac7aa7c8f7`.
- No transcript replay is required to continue; durable findings and code/test artifacts above are sufficient.

### Definition Of Done
- `release_lease` callable with identical owner/stale semantics from CLI, MCP, and HTTP.
- `board_check_out` no longer leaves orphaned leases when board entry is missing.
- `ORPHAN-TICKET` stale lease cleared and confirmed absent from `leases`.
- Relevant tests pass, with only the documented unrelated move-planner failure remaining in the broad lib sweep.
- LEASE-TICKET `ca7e0388-ee5c-41c7-8385-7de6817ce261` progressed through `in-review` to `done`.

Execute the review actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.

Next you can prepare for XSTORE-RESOLVE `f3a58d3c`

data_json.content:
I have the full picture now. Here's the preparation brief for XSTORE-RESOLVE `f3a58d3c` — grounded in the actual code paths, with the one design decision that needs your call before implementation.

## Prep Brief — XSTORE-RESOLVE `f3a58d3c`

### Root cause (confirmed in code)
The dangling check resolves targets **only in the active store's index**:

`append_dangling_edge_findings` in [health.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store/../../health.rs#L295-L326) does:
```rust
let target_exists = store.get_indexed(&edge.to).ok().flatten().is_some();
if target_exists { continue; }
// else → dangling_edge
```

- The memory-api store runs with policy `include_ancestors = false` (the default in [workspace_policy.rs](memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace_policy.rs#L57)), so the root-store targets (`82d6ada4`/`6bd67a7a`/`f8b447b7`) are **never indexed** here → flagged dangling.
- Health against `default` reports **0** because that workspace has `include_descendants = true`, so the aggregated index already contains every descendant ticket and `get_indexed` hits.

So the resolver already "works" whenever the target store is in the index; the gap is purely **ancestor (and non-indexed sibling) resolution under a store-scoped policy.**

### Available machinery
- `ticket-api` owns its discovery: `crate::workspace::discover_workspace_scan_roots_with_policy` (already used by [scan.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store/scan.rs#L137)).
- `TicketStore::open(index_root)` / `open_with` exist for opening a store read-only ([store_open.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store/store_open.rs#L98)).
- `store.index_root` is available to walk ancestors; `deny_external_paths` is the security boundary.

### Proposed approach
1. **Keep `collect_findings` signature unchanged** so CLI/MCP/HTTP parity needs zero transport edits — resolution happens inside using `store`.
2. Compute a **cross-store resolver set once per health run** (not per edge): enumerate discoverable store roots (ancestors up to repo root + descendants), bounded by `deny_external_paths`; open each read-only; cache handles. Thread it into `append_dangling_edge_findings`.
3. An edge is `dangling` only if the target resolves in **no** discoverable store.
4. **Test:** a memory-api-scoped store with a `depends_on` edge to an existing ancestor/descendant ticket produces **no** `dangling_edge`; a genuinely-absent target still flags. Add under [workflow_tests.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/tests/workflow_tests.rs) or a new health test module.

### The one decision I need from you
For **resolution** (not indexing), should we consult **ancestor stores even when `include_ancestors = false`?**

- **Yes (recommended):** the whole point is that a memory-api ticket legitimately depends on a root-store architecture ticket. Resolution should traverse the discoverable store tree (bounded by `deny_external_paths`) regardless of index-visibility policy — a policy that governs *what gets indexed/listed*, not *whether a cross-store reference is real*. This matches the ticket's "exists in NO discoverable store" acceptance criterion.
- **No (strict):** only resolve within policy-permitted stores; ancestors stay dangling unless `include_ancestors = true`. Simpler, but leaves the exact false positives the ticket targets.

I recommend **Yes**, with `deny_external_paths` as the hard security stop.

Confirm the resolution-scope decision (Yes/strict) and I'll implement — store the resolver-set builder, wire it into the dangling check, add the test, and validate that memory-api-scoped health drops from 5 danglers to 0 while a synthetic truly-missing edge still flags.

- The memory-api store runs with policy `include_ancestors = false` (the default in [workspace_policy.rs](memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace_policy.rs#L57)), so the root-store targets (`82d6ada4`/`6bd67a7a`/`f8b447b7`) are **never indexed** here → flagged dangling.

### Available machinery
- `ticket-api` owns its discovery: `crate::workspace::discover_workspace_scan_roots_with_policy` (already used by [scan.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store/scan.rs#L137)).
- `TicketStore::open(index_root)` / `open_with` exist for opening a store read-only ([store_open.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store/store_open.rs#L98)).
- `store.index_root` is available to walk ancestors; `deny_external_paths` is the security boundary.

### Proposed approach
1. **Keep `collect_findings` signature unchanged** so CLI/MCP/HTTP parity needs zero transport edits — resolution happens inside using `store`.
2. Compute a **cross-store resolver set once per health run** (not per edge): enumerate discoverable store roots (ancestors up to repo root + descendants), bounded by `deny_external_paths`; open each read-only; cache handles. Thread it into `append_dangling_edge_findings`.
3. An edge is `dangling` only if the target resolves in **no** discoverable store.
4. **Test:** a memory-api-scoped store with a `depends_on` edge to an existing ancestor/descendant ticket produces **no** `dangling_edge`; a genuinely-absent target still flags. Add under [workflow_tests.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/tests/workflow_tests.rs) or a new health test module.

### The one decision I need from you
For **resolution** (not indexing), should we consult **ancestor stores even when `include_ancestors = false`?**

- **Yes (recommended):** the whole point is that a memory-api ticket legitimately depends on a root-store architecture ticket. Resolution should traverse the discoverable store tree (bounded by `deny_external_paths`) regardless of index-visibility policy — a policy that governs *what gets indexed/listed*, not *whether a cross-store reference is real*. This matches the ticket's "exists in NO discoverable store" acceptance criterion.
- **No (strict):** only resolve within policy-permitted stores; ancestors stay dangling unless `include_ancestors = true`. Simpler, but leaves the exact false positives the ticket targets.

I recommend **Yes**, with `deny_external_paths` as the hard security stop.

fields: title=[memory-api] Cross-store edge health: shared resolver + policy-aware parent-workspace warning (parity across all stores)
arguments.description:
## Problem
Store-scoped health checks report `dangling_edge` ERRORS for `depends_on` edges that point to entities in a DIFFERENT store/workspace (e.g. memory-api tickets depending on root-store architecture tickets 82d6ada4 / 6bd67a7a / f8b447b7). These are valid cross-store URN references, not stale edges — the check resolves only within the active store's index.

## Foundation (already delivered — NOT blocked)
- `82d6ada4` URN cross-store reference model + resolver (`ce://<workspace>/<store>/<entity>`, `Urn`, `UrnResolver`) — done. Code: memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/model/urn.rs.
- `6bd67a7a` Dynamic multi-store discovery — done. Code: memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/discovery.rs (`discover_stores` — recursive, loop-safe, bounded).
- `7e318b2a` Late store onboarding reconciliation — done.
- Workspace policy (`include_descendants` / `include_ancestors` / `deny_external_paths`) — memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace_policy.rs.

## Desired behavior — three-way classification
This is a SHARED base-memory-api capability, implemented ONCE and adopted by every entity store's health check (ticket, spec, test, rule, audit) with complete parity. Given a `depends_on` edge whose target is not in the active store, resolve it across discoverable stores via the URN resolver + `discover_stores`, honoring `deny_external_paths`, then classify:

1. OK — target resolves in the active store OR a policy-INCLUDED store (e.g. an indexed descendant). No finding.
2. WARNING (`cross_workspace_edge`, severity=warning) — target resolves ONLY in a store the active workspace policy does NOT index (typically a parent/ancestor workspace when `include_ancestors = false`). Edges into non-indexed parent workspaces are DISCOURAGED: they cannot be traversed by the store's own graph queries and couple a child store to its parent. Remediate by ONE of:
   - remove/retarget the edge;
   - move the entity into a store where the edge stays intra-policy (move tooling `505b2cd4`);
   - change the workspace policy to index that ancestor (`include_ancestors = true`).
3. ERROR (`dangling_edge`, severity=error) — target resolves in NO discoverable store. Genuinely stale; remove or retarget.

## Scope
- Base layer: memory-api/crates/memory-api — shared cross-store edge resolver + classifier returning {Ok, CrossWorkspaceWarning, Dangling} plus remediation instructions. Reuse `Urn`/`UrnResolver`, `discover_stores`, and `WorkspacePolicy`. No per-store duplication.
- Per-store adoption: each store health (`ticket_api::health::collect_findings` and the spec/test/rule/audit equivalents) delegates edge classification to the shared base classifier so finding keys, severities, and messages are identical.
- Applies to CLI/MCP/HTTP uniformly (ticket-api parity via `c680b137`; mirror for other stores).

## Acceptance criteria
- Cross-store `depends_on` edges to existing entities in policy-INCLUDED stores are NOT flagged.
- Edges resolving only into a NON-indexed parent/ancestor workspace produce a `cross_workspace_edge` WARNING carrying the three remediation options — not an error, not silence.
- Only genuinely unresolvable targets remain `dangling_edge` (error).
- The classifier lives in base memory-api and is consumed by ticket + at least one other store (spec) with identical finding output (parity test).
- `deny_external_paths` is honored as the hard security boundary.
- memory-api-scoped ticket health drops from 5 danglers to 0 errors (the 5 root-store refs reclassify: existing→OK/warning, cancelled/absent→as appropriate); a synthetic truly-missing edge still flags `dangling_edge`.
- Tests: (a) edge to indexed descendant → OK; (b) edge to non-indexed ancestor → `cross_workspace_edge` warning; (c) unresolvable → `dangling_edge` error; (d) parity across ticket + spec health.

## Related
- `b03be2d5` (memory-api) cross-entity edges (spec↔ticket) — shares the base edge-resolution surface; align on one resolver, avoid a second implementation.
- `7599ed31` (memory-api) placement principle — this warning operationalizes "entities live in the lowest-owning store".
- `671d4e47` multi-store architecture tracker — umbrella.

## Blocker status
NOT blocked. Foundation (`82d6ada4` / `6bd67a7a` / `7e318b2a`) is done and the base primitives (`urn.rs`, `discovery.rs`, `workspace_policy.rs`) exist. Move tooling `505b2cd4` only enables one of three remediation paths; the warning itself does not require it.

- `82d6ada4` URN resolver (`ce://` + `Urn`/`UrnResolver`), `6bd67a7a` multi-store discovery (`discover_stores`), `7e318b2a` late-store reconciliation — all **done**. Base code exists in `model/urn.rs`, `discovery.rs`, `workspace_policy.rs`.

**Relevant planning tickets:**
- `b03be2d5` (memory-api, ready) — cross-entity edges + cross-store resolution in the base edge system (adjacent, same base layer).
- `7599ed31` (memory-api) — placement principle: entities live in lowest-owning store; the warning operationalizes exactly this.
- `671d4e47` — multi-store architecture tracker (umbrella).

The right move is to **re-scope `f3a58d3c`** from ticket-api-only into a base-memory-api shared classifier with parity across all stores and the three-way OK/warning/error model. Let me update it.

- `82d6ada4` URN cross-store reference model + resolver (`ce://<workspace>/<store>/<entity>`, `Urn`, `UrnResolver`) — done. Code: memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/model/urn.rs.
- `6bd67a7a` Dynamic multi-store discovery — done. Code: memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/discovery.rs (`discover_stores` — recursive, loop-safe, bounded).
- `7e318b2a` Late store onboarding reconciliation — done.
- Workspace policy (`include_descendants` / `include_ancestors` / `deny_external_paths`) — memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace_policy.rs.

## Desired behavior — three-way classification
This is a SHARED base-memory-api capability, implemented ONCE and adopted by every entity store's health check (ticket, spec, test, rule, audit) with complete parity. Given a `depends_on` edge whose target is not in the active store, resolve it across discoverable stores via the URN resolver + `discover_stores`, honoring `deny_external_paths`, then classify:

1. OK — target resolves in the active store OR a policy-INCLUDED store (e.g. an indexed descendant). No finding.
2. WARNING (`cross_workspace_edge`, severity=warning) — target resolves ONLY in a store the active workspace policy does NOT index (typically a parent/ancestor workspace when `include_ancestors = false`). Edges into non-indexed parent workspaces are DISCOURAGED: they cannot be traversed by the store's own graph queries and couple a child store to its parent. Remediate by ONE of:
   - remove/retarget the edge;
   - move the entity into a store where the edge stays intra-policy (move tooling `505b2cd4`);
   - change the workspace policy to index that ancestor (`include_ancestors = true`).
3. ERROR (`dangling_edge`, severity=error) — target resolves in NO discoverable store. Genuinely stale; remove or retarget.

## Scope
- Base layer: memory-api/crates/memory-api — shared cross-store edge resolver + classifier returning {Ok, CrossWorkspaceWarning, Dangling} plus remediation instructions. Reuse `Urn`/`UrnResolver`, `discover_stores`, and `WorkspacePolicy`. No per-store duplication.
- Per-store adoption: each store health (`ticket_api::health::collect_findings` and the spec/test/rule/audit equivalents) delegates edge classification to the shared base classifier so finding keys, severities, and messages are identical.
- Applies to CLI/MCP/HTTP uniformly (ticket-api parity via `c680b137`; mirror for other stores).

## Acceptance criteria
- Cross-store `depends_on` edges to existing entities in policy-INCLUDED stores are NOT flagged.
- Edges resolving only into a NON-indexed parent/ancestor workspace produce a `cross_workspace_edge` WARNING carrying the three remediation options — not an error, not silence.
- Only genuinely unresolvable targets remain `dangling_edge` (error).
- The classifier lives in base memory-api and is consumed by ticket + at least one other store (spec) with identical finding output (parity test).
- `deny_external_paths` is honored as the hard security boundary.
- memory-api-scoped ticket health drops from 5 danglers to 0 errors (the 5 root-store refs reclassify: existing→OK/warning, cancelled/absent→as appropriate); a synthetic truly-missing edge still flags `dangling_edge`.
- Tests: (a) edge to indexed descendant → OK; (b) edge to non-indexed ancestor → `cross_workspace_edge` warning; (c) unresolvable → `dangling_edge` error; (d) parity across ticket + spec health.

## Related
- `b03be2d5` (memory-api) cross-entity edges (spec↔ticket) — shares the base edge-resolution surface; align on one resolver, avoid a second implementation.
- `7599ed31` (memory-api) placement principle — this warning operationalizes "entities live in the lowest-owning store".
- `671d4e47` multi-store architecture tracker — umbrella.

## Blocker status
NOT blocked. Foundation (`82d6ada4` / `6bd67a7a` / `7e318b2a`) is done and the base primitives (`urn.rs`, `discovery.rs`, `workspace_policy.rs`) exist. Move tooling `505b2cd4` only enables one of three remediation paths; the warning itself does not require it.

events.reasoning_text:
I'm deciding whether to make f3a58d3c depend on b03be2d5 as a hard constraint or keep it as a soft relationship. Since they share the base edge-resolution surface but my feature doesn't actually need cross-entity-type edges, a hard dependency would be too restrictive—the related note in the description is sufficient. Now I need to check the ticket health.

The dependency chain looks solid: c680b137 depends on the cross-store resolver, which feeds into f3a58d3c, and that ordering makes sense. The user asked me to research and update existing tickets if implementation is blocked, which I've done for f3a58d3c. I'm considering whether b03be2d5 needs a coordination note pointing back to f3a58d3c as the shared resolver home to prevent divergent implementations, but I should be careful not to over-edit—only update what's necessary. Appending coordination note...

arguments.description:
# Cross-Entity Edges

## Objective

Extend memory-api's edge system to support edges between entities of different types (spec ↔ ticket). Currently edges are within a single entity store; this enables cross-store relationships.

## Design

Since specs and tickets share the same workspace, edges can reference UUIDs from either store. The edge system needs:

1. **Entity type annotation** on edge endpoints (spec vs ticket)
2. **Cross-store resolution** — edge validation looks up the target in the correct store
3. **Edge kind rules** for cross-entity relationships

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Edge endpoints can reference entities from different stores
- [ ] Validation checks target existence in correct store

data_json.content:
Research and ticket grooming complete. Summary:

## Research verdict — implementation is NOT blocked

**Foundation is fully delivered** (all `done`), so the base-memory-api resolver has everything it needs:
- `82d6ada4` — URN cross-store model + `UrnResolver` → [model/urn.rs](memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/model/urn.rs)
- `6bd67a7a` — recursive multi-store discovery → [discovery.rs](memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/discovery.rs) (`discover_stores`)
- `7e318b2a` — late-store onboarding reconciliation
- Workspace policy (`include_ancestors` / `deny_external_paths`) → [workspace_policy.rs](memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace_policy.rs)

No open ticket blocks the work. Move tooling `505b2cd4` only backs *one* of three remediation paths (move-entity); the warning itself doesn't require it.

## Tickets updated
- **`f3a58d3c` re-scoped** from ticket-api-only → **base memory-api shared classifier with parity across all stores**, and given your three-way model:
  - **OK** — resolves in active or policy-included store
  - **WARNING `cross_workspace_edge`** — resolves *only* in a non-indexed parent/ancestor workspace; remediate by remove-edge / move-entity / change-policy
  - **ERROR `dangling_edge`** — resolves in no discoverable store
  
  Effort bumped 1800 → 3500; title updated; added parity acceptance criteria (ticket + spec) and `deny_external_paths` boundary. Advanced `new → ready`.
- **`b03be2d5`** (cross-entity edges) — added a **coordination note** so its "cross-store resolution" reuses the *same* base resolver instead of forking a second lookup.

The warning operationalizes the placement principle already stated in `7599ed31` ("entities live in the lowest-owning store"), and `671d4e47` remains the umbrella tracker.

`f3a58d3c` is now a groomed, unblocked, ready leaf. When you want, I'll implement it: the shared classifier in base memory-api, ticket + spec health delegation, and the four-case test matrix (descendant→OK, ancestor→warning, unresolvable→error, ticket/spec parity). Want me to start the implementation, or checkpoint these ticket edits first?

Here is the full content-materialization roadmap handoff.

~~~text
# Handoff — Full Roadmap: content-materialization program (EPIC 3be95a71)

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend
- `<memory-api>` -> [./memory-api](./memory-api) — owning ticket store for EPIC and all `G-*` children (query with `workspace=<memory-api>`; note `get` against `default` fails for these ids, aggregated `subgraph`/`list` include them).
- `<root>` -> [./](./) — root context-engine ticket store (holds the done URN/discovery foundation tickets).
- `EPIC` / `G-*` / `HEALTH-PROMOTE` / `XSTORE` / `GUARD` / `SESSION-EPIC` — see Ticket Legend.

## Ticket Legend
- `EPIC` -> 3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c — "[content-materialization][epic] Fill specs, Rust/code policy, and the feedback ring on top of session construction" (effort 8000, new)
- `G-A` -> 633a38b8-7200-4979-9ce9-8c296ccc78bd — "[content-materialization][spec-api] G-A: Spec-contract v2 — motivation, dependent-expectation, computed guards, positions" (effort 2500, new; depends_on HEALTH-PROMOTE)
- `G-B` -> 17483930-36a1-4bf7-864b-e30ded445db3 — "[content-materialization][rule-api] G-B: Rust code-design policy — typed errors, trait contracts, trait inheritance" (effort 2000, new; depends_on G-B.1, G-B.2)
- `G-B.1` -> 7c281aec-092c-448d-bf26-94522e07b4e8 — "[content-materialization][memory-api] G-B.1 retro-fix: typed MoveError variant for interoperability-contract violation (replace string marker)" (effort 1500, new; leaf — smallest fully-specified)
- `G-B.2` -> fa6e2c19-5fd8-4305-90c7-f1f4bea8df36 — "[content-materialization][memory-api] G-B.2 retro-fix: trait-based interoperability contract (replace runtime validate_interoperability_contract)" (effort 2500, new; leaf)
